warnings: ABAP COMMIT WORK is implicit in Snowflake autocommit mode - transaction control differs from SAP
Hard-coded sales order ID '5000012345' should be parameterized for production use
No error handling implemented - ABAP runtime error handling not replicated
ABAP sy-datum system variable replaced with CURRENT_DATE - timezone behavior may differ
assumptions: Tables zsales_order and zsales_order_log exist in Snowflake with compatible column structures
Column data types are compatible (status as VARCHAR, dates as DATE type)
No client (MANDT) handling required based on AST analysis
Snowflake autocommit transaction mode is acceptable
Single execution context assumed (no concurrent update conflicts)
conversion_notes: Converted ABAP UPDATE statement to Snowflake UPDATE with same WHERE clause
Converted ABAP INSERT FROM VALUE to standard SQL INSERT VALUES syntax
Replaced SAP system variable sy-datum with Snowflake CURRENT_DATE function
Wrapped operations in stored procedure to maintain transactional grouping
COMMIT WORK removed as Snowflake uses autocommit by default

applied_rules.before:
REPORT z_update_order_status.

UPDATE zsales_order
SET status = 'COMPLETED',
    changed_on = sy-datum
WHERE sales_order = '5000012345'.

COMMIT WORK.



INSERT zsales_order_log FROM VALUE #(
    sales_order = '5000012345'
    action      = 'UPDATED'
    created_on  = sy-datum ).
applied_rules.after:
REPORT z_update_order_status.

UPDATE zsales_order
SET status = 'COMPLETED',
    changed_on = CURRENT_DATE
WHERE sales_order = '5000012345'.

COMMIT WORK.



INSERT zsales_order_log FROM VALUE #(
    sales_order = '5000012345'
    action      = 'UPDATED'
    created_on  = CURRENT_DATE ).
